title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope for Cancer Patients
subtitle: From faster home tests to powerful new drugs, science is changing how we fight the disease.

summary.en: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. Scientists have developed new drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for some patients. Additionally, hospitals are adopting a new, non-invasive urine test for bladder cancer. These developments represent a shift toward more manageable and less painful care.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員近期在芝加哥的一場大型會議上，分享了癌症治療的最新進展。科學家已開發出能協助免疫系統更有效地辨識並摧毀癌細胞的新藥。一種針對胰臟癌的新型藥丸，已展現出能讓部分病患存活時間加倍的效果。此外，醫院也正採用一種非侵入性的新型尿液檢測來診斷膀胱癌。這些發展代表醫療照護正朝向更易於管理且痛苦更少的方式轉變。

paragraphs.en: The fight against cancer is changing. For many years, governments have promised to find a final cure for the disease. However, as noted by The Guardian, real progress is not coming from one single "magic bullet." Instead, it is coming from small, steady steps that give patients more time to live and improve their quality of life. Recent reports from the American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting in Chicago highlight several breakthroughs that could change the future of cancer care.
paragraphs.zh: 對抗癌症的方式正在改變。多年來，各國政府承諾要找到治癒癌症的終極解方。然而，正如《衛報》（The Guardian）所指出的，真正的進展並非來自單一的「特效藥」。相反地，進展來自於穩定的小步快跑，讓病患能爭取更多存活時間並改善生活品質。近期在芝加哥舉行的美國臨床腫瘤學會（American Society of Clinical Oncology）會議報告，強調了幾項可能改變未來癌症照護的突破性進展。

paragraphs.en: One of the most significant developments is a new drug called daraxonrasib. Pancreatic cancer is known for being very difficult to treat, and survival rates are usually low. In a recent clinical trial, this new medicine helped patients live twice as long as they would have with older treatments. While the increase in time might seem small—moving from six months to 13 months on average—it is incredibly important for patients and their families. Experts believe this drug could eventually help treat other types of cancer as well, because it targets a specific group of molecules that often cause cells to grow out of control.
paragraphs.zh: 其中一項最重要的發展是一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥。胰臟癌以難以治療著稱，存活率通常很低。在近期的一項臨床試驗中，這種新藥幫助病患的存活時間比使用舊有療法延長了一倍。雖然時間的增加看起來不多——平均從六個月延長至十三個月——但對於病患及其家屬而言，這意義重大。專家認為，這種藥物最終也能協助治療其他類型的癌症，因為它鎖定了一組常導致細胞失控生長的特定分子。

paragraphs.en: Beyond new drugs, there is also progress in how doctors find cancer. In the United Kingdom, hospitals are starting to use a new, faster way to test for bladder cancer. Traditionally, doctors used a method called a cystoscopy. This involves using a thin tube with a camera to look inside the bladder. It is an invasive procedure—meaning it requires entering the body—and many patients find it uncomfortable or embarrassing. Because of this, some people choose not to have the test at all.
paragraphs.zh: 除了新藥之外，醫師診斷癌症的方式也有所進展。在英國，醫院正開始採用一種檢測膀胱癌的新型快速方法。傳統上，醫師使用一種稱為膀胱鏡檢查的方法，這需要將一根帶有攝影機的細管伸入膀胱內部。這是一種侵入性程序——意即需要進入人體——許多病患會感到不適或尷尬。正因如此，有些人選擇完全不做檢查。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the new Galeas bladder test is a major improvement. It is a simple urine test that patients can take at home. Because it is painless and convenient, more people are willing to be tested. Early data from the Leicester NHS trust shows that this new method is not only more comfortable but also faster, helping patients get their results well within the standard 28-day target. In a recent trial, the test correctly identified cancer in 92% of cases, which is more accurate than the traditional camera method.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》報導，新型的 Galeas 膀胱檢測是一項重大改進。這是一種簡單的尿液檢測，病患可以在家中自行採樣。由於過程無痛且方便，更多人願意接受檢測。來自萊斯特國民保健署（Leicester NHS trust）的早期數據顯示，這種新方法不僅更舒適，速度也更快，能協助病患在標準的二十八天目標內取得結果。在近期的一項試驗中，該檢測準確辨識出百分之九十二的癌症病例，準確度高於傳統的攝影機檢查法。

paragraphs.en: These advances show how modern medicine is moving toward more personalized care. Genetic screening—the process of checking a person’s DNA to find specific health risks—has become a routine part of cancer medicine. By identifying which patients have specific genetic mutations, doctors can choose the right medicine for the right person. This helps avoid unnecessary treatments and makes sure that patients get the most effective care available.
paragraphs.zh: 這些進展顯示現代醫學正朝向更個人化的照護邁進。基因篩檢——即檢查個人去氧核糖核酸（DNA）以找出特定健康風險的過程——已成為癌症醫療的常規部分。透過辨識哪些病患帶有特定的基因突變，醫師能為合適的人選擇合適的藥物。這有助於避免不必要的治療，並確保病患獲得目前最有效的照護。

paragraphs.en: Michelle Mitchell, the head of Cancer Research UK, described this period as a “golden age for cancer research.” While we may never fully defeat cancer, the ability to turn it into a manageable condition is becoming a reality. Much like how HIV was once a death sentence but is now a condition that people can live with for many years, researchers hope to do the same for various types of cancer. By building on these small successes, scientists are slowly changing the outlook for millions of people around the world.
paragraphs.zh: 英國癌症研究協會（Cancer Research UK）負責人蜜雪兒·米契爾（Michelle Mitchell）將此時期形容為「癌症研究的黃金時代」。雖然我們可能永遠無法徹底擊敗癌症，但將其轉變為可控疾病的能力正逐漸成為現實。就像人類免疫缺乏病毒（HIV）曾被視為絕症，如今卻已成為人們能與之共存多年的慢性病，研究人員希望對各類癌症也能達成同樣的目標。透過累積這些小小的成功，科學家正緩慢地改變全球數百萬人的前景。

paragraphs.en: However, experts warn that we must be patient. Scientific progress is often slow, and what seems impossible today can become standard practice tomorrow. The success of these new treatments depends on continued research and the willingness of health systems to adopt new technologies. As more hospitals begin to use the Galeas test and more trials for drugs like daraxonrasib continue, the hope is that these small advances will add up to a much brighter future. For now, the focus remains on winning more time, more remissions—a period where the disease is inactive—and more life for those facing a cancer diagnosis.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，專家警告我們必須保持耐心。科學進展往往緩慢，今日看似不可能的事，明日可能成為標準做法。這些新療法的成功取決於持續的研究，以及醫療體系採用新技術的意願。隨著更多醫院開始使用 Galeas 檢測，且針對如 daraxonrasib 等藥物的試驗持續進行，人們期望這些小小的進展能匯聚成更光明的未來。目前，重點仍放在為面對癌症診斷的病患爭取更多時間、更多緩解期——即疾病處於不活躍的時期——以及更多生命。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A simple, perfect solution to a complex problem.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 萬靈丹；解決複雜問題的簡單完美方案。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個隱喻用法，字面上是「魔法子彈」，但在醫學或科學語境中指能解決所有問題的特效藥。文中：real progress is not coming from one single "magic bullet."

keyTerms.definitionEn: No longer under restraint or supervision; impossible to manage.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡形容癌細胞生長不受身體機制限制。文中：because it targets a specific group of molecules that often cause cells to grow out of control.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation that will inevitably lead to death or failure.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意為法律上的「死刑判決」，這裡引申為「無法治癒的絕症」。文中：Much like how HIV was once a death sentence but is now a condition that people can live with for many years, researchers hope to do the same for various types of cancer.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To result in a particular total or outcome.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 總結為；最終導致某種結果。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於數學（加總），這裡指許多小進步累積成最終的巨大成果。文中：the hope is that these small advances will add up to a much brighter future.

sources.title: NHS hospitals adopt faster, more accurate at-home bladder cancer test

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Hope in the Fight Against Cancer
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. Scientists have developed new drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. One new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for patients. Additionally, hospitals in the UK are starting to use a new, non-invasive test for bladder cancer. These advancements represent a significant step forward in making cancer treatment more effective and comfortable for patients.

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope in Cancer Fight
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. These new drugs work by helping the immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. One specific pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. Another treatment helps patients with various common cancers by removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that tumors use to hide. While these results are early, experts believe they represent a significant step forward in oncology.

previousArticles.title: New Advances Bring Hope in the Fight Against Cancer
previousArticles.summaryEn: Recent medical conferences and studies have revealed major progress in cancer treatment. Scientists have developed new smart drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for patients. Surgical research suggests that choosing the right method can significantly lower the risk of cervical cancer returning. However, experts warn that a global shortage of healthcare workers could create future challenges for cancer care.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is a specific benefit of the new Galeas bladder test compared to the traditional cystoscopy?

options.A: It is significantly more expensive but provides faster results.
options.B: It is a non-invasive procedure that can be performed at home.
options.C: It is 100% accurate in detecting all cases of bladder cancer.
options.D: It requires a specialized camera to be inserted into the body.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第四段明確指出 Galeas 膀胱檢測是一種簡單的尿液檢測，患者可以在家中進行，且不會感到疼痛。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提及費用；選項 C 錯誤，文中指出準確率為 92% 而非 100%；選項 D 描述的是傳統的 cystoscopy（膀胱鏡檢查），而非新的 Galeas 檢測。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future of cancer treatment based on the article's comparison to HIV?

options.A: Researchers believe that cancer will soon be eradicated completely.
options.B: Cancer treatment will focus primarily on finding a single 'magic bullet'.
options.C: The goal is shifting from achieving a total cure to managing cancer as a long-term condition.
options.D: Genetic screening will eventually replace the need for all drug-based cancer treatments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段提到，研究人員希望將癌症轉變為一種「可管理的疾病」，就像 HIV 一樣，從原本的絕症變為可以長期共存的狀態。選項 A 與文中「我們可能永遠無法完全戰勝癌症」的觀點矛盾；選項 B 與第一段提到的「不再依賴單一魔彈」觀點相反；選項 D 在文中並未提及，基因篩檢是輔助手段而非替代藥物的方案。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'remissions' mean in the context of cancer care?

options.A: A state where the disease is not currently showing symptoms or activity.
options.B: A permanent cure that prevents the cancer from ever returning.
options.C: The process of testing new drugs on patients in a hospital setting.
options.D: A period of time where the patient undergoes invasive surgery.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中括號內明確解釋了 remissions 為「a period where the disease is inactive」（疾病處於不活躍狀態的時期）。選項 B 錯誤，remission 並不等於完全治癒；選項 C 錯誤，這是指臨床試驗；選項 D 錯誤，這與手術過程無關。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding the fight against cancer?

options.A: Technological breakthroughs have made cancer a simple disease to treat.
options.B: The most effective way to fight cancer is by increasing government funding for research.
options.C: Modern medicine is making incremental progress by focusing on personalized care and better quality of life.
options.D: Patients should stop relying on traditional hospital treatments and use home-based tests instead.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章的核心主旨貫穿全文，強調癌症治療正透過個人化醫療、新藥物與檢測技術的「小步快跑」來改善患者的生活品質，而非尋找單一的萬靈丹。選項 A 過於誇大；選項 B 文中雖有提及研究的重要性，但非核心主旨；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未建議患者放棄傳統醫療，而是強調新技術的輔助與整合。
